data.name: Terminology_Encounter
data.value: Encounter: An encounter is any fight in the game. That includes gimmick fights such as Sophie’s dodge tutorial in Lumiere or helping the white Troubadour learn to play.

data.name: Terminology_Encounter size
data.value: Encounter size: The number of enemies in the encounter.

data.name: Terminology_EnemyArchetype
data.value: Enemy archetype: Internally, all enemies are divided into eight types called archetypes, which govern their stats. These archetypes include four regular enemy types - Elusive (flying), Weak, Strong, and Regular, as well as Elite (miniboss), Boss, Alpha (chromatic), and Petank.

data.name: Terminology_EnemyCategory
data.value: Enemy category: These were created by me to make it easier to work with the mod. You can find the full list in the mod.

data.name: Terminology_EnemyLoot
data.value: Enemy loot: All drops that are seen in the Victory screen are tied specifically to the enemies you’ve defeated in the fight, and not the encounter itself. Some of these drops are invisible, such as the item that any merchant drops after you defeat them in battle that allows you to buy the special inventory. Another notable example is Maelle’s paintress skills unlock, which is considered a loot drop from the Paintress enemy.

data.name: Terminology_Check
data.value: Check: Anything in the game that results in the player getting an item. Examples include enemy drops, map pick-ups, or items obtained in a dialogue, among others

data.name: Terminology_CheckSizeNumber
data.value: Check size/number of items: The number of individual items in the check.

data.name: Terminology_ItemQuantity
data.value: Item quantity: Some items, such as Chroma Catalysts or Recoat can have their quantities configured in the randomizer. An example for both of these concepts would be if a Lancelier got randomized to drop a Baguette, a Swimsuit, a Bald haircut, and 5 Perfect Chroma Catalysts - the check size is 4, and the quantities of the items are 1, 1, 1, and 5.

data.name: Terminology_MerchantUnlocks
data.value: Merchant unlocks: Most merchants in the game have some items they are only willing to sell after you’ve defeated them in battle. Internally, this works by checking whether you have a hidden item that drops from the merchant enemy. These items are called Merchant Unlocks and are a separate category, not randomized by default.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_RandomizeEnemies
data.value: Randomize enemies

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_RandomizeEnemies
data.value: Whether or not the enemies are randomized.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_RandomizeEncounterSizes
data.value: Randomize encounter sizes

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_RandomizeEncounterSizes
data.value: This allows you to override whatever the base number of enemies in an encounter is. As an example, if you select 1 and 3 but not 2, it will make it so that regardless of initial encounter size, it will become either 1 or 3.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_ChangeSizeOfEncounters
data.value: Randomize size of encounters that don't contain randomized enemies.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ChangeSizeOfEncounters
data.value: If Randomize encounter sizes option is turned on, this will allow you to only change the size of the encounters that you want to randomize. For example, if you want to randomize most of the encounters, but don’t want to change the tutorial Maelle fight, keep this option unchecked to not add any more enemies to the encounter.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_RandomizeMerchantFights
data.value: Randomize merchant fights

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_RandomizeMerchantFights
data.value: This allows you to control whether the “Fight the Merchant” encounters are at all randomized. This affects the whole encounter, not just the merchant enemy itself.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_RandomizeMerchantFights_Note
data.value: Merchants' special wares unlocks and Maelle's paintress skills are considered loot drops. It is recommended that you only disable this if you are also running an item randomizer.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_IncludeCutContent
data.value: Include cut content (gets overriden by custom placement)

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_IncludeCutContent
data.value: The game files actually contain a lot of fully-functional cut content enemies, so turning this on will enable you to fight them. It should be noted that while I tried my best to exclude any enemies that completely break the game or make it crash, it is always a risk when dealing with cut content. If anything goes wrong, please use the “Edit Individual Encounter” window to replace them with something that works. Also, be careful when parrying any cut content enemies, but especially Gestral Bullies and Dragoons, as it may cause their AI to freeze.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_IncludeCutContent_Note
data.value: Be careful when parrying cut content enemies, as it can break their AI. You can replace broken enemies in Edit Encounters window.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnableEnemyOnslaught
data.value: Enable enemy onslaught

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_EnableEnemyOnslaught
data.value: This option allows you to add up to 99 additional enemies to every encounter. The game actually has full support for 4-enemy encounters, but anything beyond that will just place all the other enemies in a single spot outside the fighting arena. They will still be able to deal and take damage, but their attacks will not be visible, and you won’t be able to shoot them in free aim. If you choose not to randomize additional enemies, they will just repeat until the set number is reached. Otherwise, they will get randomized after being copied, which makes it so that the new enemies use the same rules as the starting ones.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnableEnemyOnslaught_Note
data.value: The game has full support for 4 enemies, but the 5th and beyond gets lumped into a single spot outside the fighting arena. They will still function properly except that you won't be able to see their attacks or shoot them.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnableEnemyOnslaught_RandomizeAddedEnemies
data.value: Randomize added enemies

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnableEnemyOnslaught_NumberOfAdditionalEnemies
data.value: Number of additional enemies:

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnableEnemyOnslaught_CapEncounterSizesAt
data.value: Cap encounter size at:

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_EnsureEncountersAtLeastOne
data.value: Ensure that boss encounters have at least one boss

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_EnsureEncountersAtLeastOne
data.value: This option makes it so that the encounters that contained a boss enemy in the base game will still contain at least one boss enemy after randomizing. This rule is applied after the initial randomization and ignores Custom Enemy Placement rules.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_ReduceBossRepetition
data.value: Reduce boss repetition (ignores frequency adjustments)

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReduceBossRepetition
data.value: This option makes it so that whenever a boss enemy is rolled, it is taken from a shuffled pool of bosses rather than completely randomly. This rule is applied after the initial randomization and ignores Custom Enemy Placement rules.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_ReplacePhase2Simon
data.value: Replace Phase 2 Simon with Phase 1 in mandatory fights until after the Lune tutorial fight

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2Simon
data.value: As Phase 2 Simon has a unique property of wiping your whole party with no counterplay, as well as six times more health than a regular boss would have in his place, this option ensures that you can theoretically beat the game in NG with 99 Simons in all encounters. Enemies like Clea or Bourgeon can also block your progress, so feel free to use the “Edit Individual Encounter” window to replace them.

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter
data.value: Replace Phase 2 Simon with Phase 1 in mandatory fights until after:

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_Lune
data.value: Lune Tutorial

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_Eveque
data.value: Eveque

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_Goblu
data.value: Goblu

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_UltimateSakapatate
data.value: Ultimate Sakapatate

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_GestralTournament
data.value: Gestral Tournament

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Tooltip_ReplacePhase2SimonUntilAfter_Never
data.value: Never

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Button_EditEnemyPlacements
data.value: Edit Enemy Placements

data.name: Randomize_Enemies_Button_EditIndividualEncounters
data.value: Edit Individual Encounters

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeItems
data.value: Randomize items

data.name: Randomize_Items_MakeEveryItemShowAPickUpNotification
data.value: Make every item show a pick up notification

data.name: Randomize_Items_IncludeCutContentItems
data.value: Include cut content items

data.name: Randomize_Items_ReceivePaintedPowerAtTheStartOfActIII
data.value: Receive Painted Power at the start of Act III

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeStartingWeapons
data.value: Randomize starting weapons

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeStartingCosmetics
data.value: Randomize starting cosmetics

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeEsquiesRocksIntoMapPickups
data.value: Randomize Esquie's rocks into map pickups

data.name: Randomize_Items_LimitEsquiesRocksToOnlyTheirOriginalAreas
data.value: Limit Esquie's rocks to only their original areas

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeEnemyFeetDrops
data.value: Randomize enemy feet drops

data.name: Randomize_Items_EnableGearRollsInPrologue
data.value: Enable gear rolls in prologue (they will be level 16 and can drastically alter the difficulty)

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeGestralBeachRewards
data.value: Randomize gestral beach rewards

data.name: Randomize_Items_ChangeNumberOfItemsInNon-randomizedChecks
data.value: Change number of items in non-randomized checks

data.name: Randomize_Items_ReduceKeyItemRepetition
data.value: Reduce key item repetition

data.name: Randomize_Items_ReduceGearRepetition
data.value: Reduce gear repetition

data.name: Randomize_Items_MakeAnyOfTheMerchantWaresLockedWithProbability
data.value: Make any of the merchant wares locked with probability

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeMerchantInventorySizesBetween
data.value: Randomize merchant inventory sizes between

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeNumberOfLootDropsBetween
data.value: Randomize number of loot drops between

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeNumberOfTheEndlessTowerRewardsBetween
data.value: Randomize number of the Endless Tower rewards between

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeNumberOfItemsPerMapPickupBetween
data.value: Randomize number of items per map pickup between

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeNumberOfCutsceneRewardsBetween
data.value: Randomize number of cutscene rewards between

data.name: Randomize_Items_RandomizeQuantitiesOfUpgradeMaterialsBetween
data.value: Randomize quantities of upgrade materials between

data.name: Randomize_Items_CustomizeItemPlacements
data.value: Customize Item Placements

data.name: Randomize_Items_EditIndividualChecks
data.value: Edit Individual Checks

data.name: Main_RandomizeItems
data.value: Randomize items: Whether or not the items are randomized.

data.name: Main_MakeEveryItemShowNotification
data.value: Make every item show a pick up notification: A lot of items are hidden by default, which could lead to confusion as to what the player actually gets if they are randomized. It is strongly recommended to keep this option on.

data.name: Main_IncludeCutContentItems
data.value: Include cut content items: There are about 20 cut content pictos and 14 cut content weapons with varying degrees of functionality, but none of them can break the game or anything. Still, if you do not wish to see them, turn this option off.

data.name: Main_ReceivePaintedPowerActIII
data.value: Receive Painted Power at the start of Act III: Since the enemies’ drops are now randomized, it is no longer guaranteed that the player will get Painted Power from the last fight of Act II. As such, this option ensures that the player gets it regardless.

data.name: Main_RandomizeStartingWeaponsCosmetics
data.value: Randomize starting weapons/cosmetics: Whether the starting equipment of your characters should be random too. This ignores custom item placement.

data.name: Main_RandomizeEsquieRocksMapPickups
data.value: Randomize Esquie’s rocks into map pickups: This option makes it so that you now need to find the rock items scattered on the map before Esquie can use his abilities. The rocks can only be found as a pickup wherever a unique item (weapon, pictos, etc) was originally present. You need to go to camp with the rock in your inventory to unlock them.

data.name: Main_LimitEsquiesRocksOriginalAreas
data.value: Limit Esquie’s rocks to only their original areas: With this off, the rocks can be found in the following areas: Florrie - Spring Meadows, Flying Waters, Ancient Sanctuary, Esquie’s Nest, Stone Wave Cliffs. Dorrie - Forgotten Battlefield, Old Lumiere. Soarrie - Visages, Sirene, Monolith. Otherwise, the rocks can only be found in the areas they were found in the original game - Stone Wave Cliffs, Old Lumiere, and Monolith, respectively.

data.name: Main_RandomizeEnemyFeetDrops
data.value: Randomize enemy feet drops: While the feet are their own items that can be encountered normally, the underlying game system guaranteed their drops from their respective owners. This setting mixes up the type of feet that the enemies drop - a Chalier can now give Monoco Lampmaster's Sword, for example.

data.name: Main_EnableGearRollsInPrologue
data.value: Enable gear rolls in prologue: Due to how the game areas work, the items in the prologue are considered level 16 internally, the same as Lumiere Act III. This option exists to prevent the balance of the game from being completely broken in case the player gets a level 16 weapon or pictos in the prologue. Internally, the mod checks whether the drop would have been a weapon/pictos, and then replaces it with a Chroma Catalyst.

data.name: Main_RandomizeGestralBeachRewards
data.value: Randomize gestral beach rewards: Some players understandably prefer not to engage with the gestral beaches, especially if the rewards are not guaranteed to be good. This option exists to prevent them from worrying about it (but not from being silently judged).

data.name: Main_ChangeNumberItemsNonRandomizedChecks
data.value: Change number of items in non-randomized checks: Similar to the option in the enemy randomizer, this option makes it so that the checks that were configured not to be changed don’t award multiple of the same item.

data.name: Main_ReduceKeyItemRepetition
data.value: Reduce key item repetition: This option makes it so that whenever a key item is rolled, it is taken from a shuffled pool of items rather than completely randomly. This rule is applied after the initial randomization and ignores Custom Item Placement rules.

data.name: Main_MakeAnyMerchantWaresLocked
data.value: Make any of the merchant wares locked: This option makes it so that instead of merchants using their base-game locked item statuses, they have them locked with a certain probability. For example, base game Noco has only his first inventory item locked, but if this option is set to 33%, one third of all his items will be locked on average.

data.name: Main_RandomizeCheckSizes
data.value: Randomize various check sizes: The set of options changes different checks’ sizes, such as the number of items in a merchant’s inventory or the number of items that are awarded on picking up a shiny on the map.

data.name: Main_RandomizeQuantitiesUpgradeMaterials
data.value: Randomize quantities of upgrade materials: If this option is on, any time the item is rolled to be an upgrade material, its quantity will be randomized. The only items whose quantity can be greater than 1 are: Chroma Catalysts, Color of Lumina, and Recoat.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_NotRandomized
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies aren’t randomized, meaning that they will stay in their respective encounters.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_Excluded
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies are excluded from the pool, meaning that they will not occur anywhere in the randomized game.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_QuickPresets
data.value: This allows you to select the Custom Enemy Placement rules from a list of quick presets. All presets are declared and can be configured in the Data/Presets/[preset name].json file. The Oops All preset replaces every enemy in the game with the chosen option, while the default Split Categories preset randomizes all regular enemies into other regular ones, all the bosses into other bosses, etc.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_CustomPlacement
data.value: In order to use this, you need to first select the enemy that is going to be replaced on the left, then select what it will be replaced by on the right. For example, the “10% of regular enemies are bosses” preset will give the Regular enemies category a 90% chance to become another Regular enemy and a 10% chance to become a Boss enemy. The numbers don’t have to add up to 100; they just represent the weights for different categories.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_FrequencyAdjustment
data.value: This allows you to tweak how often a specific enemy is encountered in the randomizer. For example, if you want to see fewer Bourgeons in the run but keep all the other settings the same, you can just adjust their frequency to 10% in this section, making them 10 times less likely to appear.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Enemy_Title
data.value: Custom Enemy Placement

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_NotRandomized
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies aren’t randomized, meaning that they will stay in their respective encounters.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_Excluded
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies are excluded from the pool, meaning that they will not occur anywhere in the randomized game.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_QuickPresets
data.value: This allows you to select the Custom Item Placement rules from a list of quick presets. All presets are declared and can be configured in the Data/Presets/[preset name].json file. The Oops All preset replaces every Item in the game with the chosen option, while the default Split Categories preset randomizes all regular enemies into other regular ones, all the bosses into other bosses, etc.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_CustomPlacement
data.value: In order to use this, you need to first select the Item that is going to be replaced on the left, then select what it will be replaced by on the right. For example, the “10% of regular enemies are bosses” preset will give the Regular enemies category a 90% chance to become another Regular Item and a 10% chance to become a Boss Item. The numbers don’t have to add up to 100; they just represent the weights for different categories.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_FrequencyAdjustment
data.value: This allows you to tweak how often a specific Item is encountered in the randomizer. For example, if you want to see fewer Bourgeons in the run but keep all the other settings the same, you can just adjust their frequency to 10% in this section, making them 10 times less likely to appear.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Item_Title
data.value: Custom Item Placement

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_NotRandomized
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies aren’t randomized, meaning that they will stay in their respective encounters.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_Excluded
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies are excluded from the pool, meaning that they will not occur anywhere in the randomized game.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_QuickPresets
data.value: This allows you to select the Custom Skill Placement rules from a list of quick presets. All presets are declared and can be configured in the Data/Presets/[preset name].json file. The Oops All preset replaces every Skill in the game with the chosen option, while the default Split Categories preset randomizes all regular enemies into other regular ones, all the bosses into other bosses, etc.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_CustomPlacement
data.value: In order to use this, you need to first select the Skill that is going to be replaced on the left, then select what it will be replaced by on the right. For example, the “10% of regular enemies are bosses” preset will give the Regular enemies category a 90% chance to become another Regular Skill and a 10% chance to become a Boss Skill. The numbers don’t have to add up to 100; they just represent the weights for different categories.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_FrequencyAdjustment
data.value: This allows you to tweak how often a specific Skill is encountered in the randomizer. For example, if you want to see fewer Bourgeons in the run but keep all the other settings the same, you can just adjust their frequency to 10% in this section, making them 10 times less likely to appear.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Skill_Title
data.value: Custom Skill Placement

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_NotRandomized
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies aren’t randomized, meaning that they will stay in their respective encounters.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_Excluded
data.value: This allows you to change which enemies are excluded from the pool, meaning that they will not occur anywhere in the randomized game.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_QuickPresets
data.value: This allows you to select the Custom Location Placement rules from a list of quick presets. All presets are declared and can be configured in the Data/Presets/[preset name].json file. The Oops All preset replaces every Location in the game with the chosen option, while the default Split Categories preset randomizes all regular enemies into other regular ones, all the bosses into other bosses, etc.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_CustomPlacement
data.value: In order to use this, you need to first select the Location that is going to be replaced on the left, then select what it will be replaced by on the right. For example, the “10% of regular enemies are bosses” preset will give the Regular enemies category a 90% chance to become another Regular Location and a 10% chance to become a Boss Location. The numbers don’t have to add up to 100; they just represent the weights for different categories.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_FrequencyAdjustment
data.value: This allows you to tweak how often a specific Location is encountered in the randomizer. For example, if you want to see fewer Bourgeons in the run but keep all the other settings the same, you can just adjust their frequency to 10% in this section, making them 10 times less likely to appear.

data.name: CustomPlacement_Location_Title
data.value: Custom Location Placement

data.name: CustomPlacement_PresetJSON
data.value: Preset JSON: This shows you the current configuration in brief and allows you to copy it if you want to.

data.name: CustomPlacement_LoadSavePresetJSON
data.value: Load/Save Preset JSON: These buttons allow you to load and save the current configuration as a .json file, making it easy to share and save your presets.

data.name: ToolTip_Header_HowItWorks
data.value: How It Works

data.name: CustomPlacement_Header_NotRandomized
data.value: Not Randomized

data.name: CustomPlacement_Header_Excluded
data.value: Excluded

data.name: SearchPlaceholder
data.value: Search...

data.name: CustomPlacement_Header_SelectCategory
data.value: Select Category

data.name: CustomPlacement_Header_IsReplacedBy
data.value: Is Replaced By

data.name: NothingSelected
data.value: Nothing Selected

data.name: ToolTip_Remove
data.value: Remove

data.name: CustomPlacement_Button_AddPlacementRow
data.value: Add Placement Row

data.name: CustomPlacement_Button_AddFrequencyRow
data.value: Add Frequency Row

data.name: CustomPlacement_TabItem_AddCustomPlacements
data.value: Add Custom Placements

data.name: CustomPlacement_TabItem_SpecialRules
data.value: Special Rules

data.name: CustomPlacement_TabItem_FrequencyAdjustments
data.value: Frequency Adjustments

data.name: CustomPlacement_Header_Adjustments
data.value: Adjustments

data.name: CustomPlacement_Button_QuickPresets
data.value: Quick Presets

data.name: CustomPlacement_Button_LoadPresetJson
data.value: Load Preset JSON

data.name: CustomPlacement_Button_SavePresetJson
data.value: Save Preset JSON

data.name: Button_Close
data.value: Close
